event: Flying Bomb Depot. The Caverns, Saint Leu D'Esserent
symbols: cavern — the hidden underworld of war and concealed destruction
beam of light from above — penetration of truth into hidden places of darkness
scattered debris — the chaos and disorder wrought by human violence
deep shadows — moral darkness and the concealment of instruments of death
yellow-ochre walls — the ancient limestone quarry repurposed for destructive ends
description: A dark, cavernous interior rendered in muted tones of ochre, brown, and black. A shaft of light descends from the upper portion of the composition, illuminating scattered mechanical debris, crates, and rubble on the cavern floor. The thick stone walls frame the scene with heavy, oppressive mass. The inscription '14.1.45 Sutherland' and 'St Leu d'Esserent' appear at the bottom. The atmosphere is one of desolation and eerie abandonment, depicting a former V-1 flying bomb storage depot discovered by Allied forces in the limestone quarries of northern France.

_meta.title: Flying Bomb Depot. The Caverns, Saint Leu D'Esserent
